code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment of the review based solely on the review text
    - Base the rating on overall customer satisfaction, not on isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1 Star (Very Negative):
    - Complete functional failure despite proper usage attempts
    - Multi-purchase defects with safety hazards
    - Usability failures making product impractical despite concept appeal
    - Negative updates overriding initial positive experiences

    2 Stars (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Persistent frustration despite occasional success
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Waste of money" declarations with justification

    3 Stars (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...") without endorsement
    - Technical competence with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without enthusiasm

    4 Stars (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility (e.g. sizing/durability)
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only reason not 5 stars...")
    - Requires break-in period for full functionality
    - Assembly difficulties despite final satisfaction
    - Brief factual praise without enthusiastic language

    5 Stars (Very Positive):
    - 3+ complementary aspects praised (functionality + design + durability)
    - Close comparison to top brands with satisfaction despite minor differences
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core functional praise
    - Solves persistent problems from previous solutions
    - Concise praise without negatives ("Fits perfectly")
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" endorsements despite imperfections
    - Long-term validation (>6mo) with sustained performance

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Functional praise + functional criticism →4
    - Functional praise + non-functional criticism →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - "Nearly matches X" comparisons →5 if satisfied with performance
    - Brief positivity without enthusiasm →4 ("does the job")
    - Core functionality excellence post-setup →5 despite initial effort
    - Resolved issues →5 if no lingering functional impact
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures override any positives →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Perfect fit! Does exactly what I needed."
<Reasoning>Concise enthusiastic praise without negatives →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Not quite Lodge quality but very close. Excellent daily performer!"
<Reasoning>Close brand comparison with satisfaction →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Innovative concept fails - must remove items for basic use."
<Reasoning>Core usability failure despite good concept →1.</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "These potholders work well after washing smell out. Stiff edges remain."
<Reasoning>Lingering functional drawback post-resolution →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Surprisingly useful long-term! Handles puppy+kid messes effectively."
<Reasoning>Solves persistent problem exceeding expectations →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Fits mattresses adequately. Does the job."
<Reasoning>Factual description lacking enthusiasm →4.</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Material feels cheap but holds items securely."
<Reasoning>Functional adequacy with quality compromise →3.</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Constantly retighten screws - not worth the effort."
<Reasoning>Excessive maintenance for basic function →2.</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Vibrant colors need ironing but worth it for durability."
<Reasoning>Non-functional criticism with core praise →5.</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (27 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 12
  gold 5 -> 4: 7
  gold 4 -> 3: 2
  gold 2 -> 3: 1
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "I can forgive the fact that there was blood on one of the math mats when I opened the package.  Things happen and I'm sorry that someone was injured.  What I can't get past is the fact that there are so many snags.  It's like these two mats were seco..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 2] | Disagreement: 0.50
  2. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "Between a puppy and frequent visits from children, I've definitely had times when it felt like the gap between proper carpet cleanings was entirely too long. On the other hand, I never entirely bought the idea that a device of this type could turn ou..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "I have only used this once so far, but I really like it. It is easy to operate once you figure out the steam buttons...lol! The scrubber was effective and it cleaned a pretty sticky kitchen floor. Next..on to my tile."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I'm partial to the Presto Pop Lite hot air popper, but it looks cheap and plasticy, so I tried this.

All in all this works like the Presto or any other hot air popper, but it has a couple nuances (good and bad):

This unit is more compact and attrac..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "This is a good, though not extra ordinary storage bin. It lacks exceptional build quality, exceptional attention to detail, or anything that's all that remarkable. It looks good, and the pattern is timeless, plus it keeps stuff together. You could do..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "I got this low profile box spring for my office to use along with a Sealy Cocoon mattress, which is soft. After putting this one together, I will need to add a piece of wood to support the mattress so that it doesn't dip between the slats. The slats ..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33

Summary: 27 errors, 21 borderline out of 80 examples."}
